input.background: Substitute the appropriate values of the dummy variables in model 3 to obtain the equations of the three response lines in Figure 12.23.
input.question: What is the complete model that characterizes the three lines in Figure 12.23?

output.answer: The complete model that characterizes the three lines in Figure 12.23 is $$E(y)=\beta_0+\beta_1 x_1+\beta_2 x_2+\beta_3 x_3+\beta_4 x_1 x_2+\beta_5 x_1 x_3$$ where $$\begin{aligned} & x_1=\text { advertising expenditure } \\ & x_2= \begin{cases}1 & \text { if radio medium } \\ 0 & \text { if not }\end{cases} \\ & x_3= \begin{cases}1 & \text { if television medium } \\ 0 & \text { if not }\end{cases} \end{aligned}$$

meta_info.caption: Interpreting the $\\beta$ 's in a Model with Mixed Independent Variables
meta_info.section: 12.8 Models with Both Quantitative and Qualitative Variables
